observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
12. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continuously affect the timely and accurate mandatory contributions to GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG, and income tax within the prescribed rate and period of remittances;
13. The discrepancies from the previous years’ related accounts per financial statement must be thoroughly reviewed and reconciled to present the accurate status of contribution and remittance; and
14. The employees’ ledger cards must be set up completely and regularly maintained for easier reconciliation and monitoring of the actual status of accounts of LGUs and the employee’s shares.

We recommended that Management furnish a COA copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA 2009-001 within 5-days after its perfection/approval/execution for proper evaluation and remedial measures on possible defects will not be too late to enforce.

It is recommended that the administration:
36. Must make sure that the conditions and requirements prescribed in Appendix I of RA 9184 are duly complied with before the implementation/construction of any project to be undertaken by administration;
37. Must provide campaign strategies designed at promoting a positive image of the Municipality where peace and order are stable and people are hospitable; and
38. Install safety and security measures so that the life and property of any prospective contractors can properly be safeguarded.

We recommended that time management should ensure that due notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time under COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the conduct of a thorough and complete physical count of
all properties of the Municipality; and
b) Require the submission of a complete report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RCPPE).

Hence, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct that:
a) The drawing of cash advances for salaries be strictly equal to the
net amount of the payroll for a pay period and shall not be drawn
without the supporting payroll;
b) The excess of cash advances granted be refunded immediately by
the issuance of an Official Receipt and depositing thereof intact to
the depository bank;
c) The cash advance shall only be used for the specific purpose for
which it was granted.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the concerned key
officials to faithfully observe the rules and regulations in the granting,
utilization, and liquidation of cash advances, specifically the following:
a) Cash Advance shall be reported to the Municipal Accountant as
soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served;
b) The Municipal Accountant shall ensure that all cash advances are
strictly monitored, liquidated, and properly booked within the
prescribed period.

Thus, we recommended that the LCE mandate the concerned officials
to faithfully observe the following courses of action in the preparation
and submission of the financial reports and statements, viz:
a. Strictly adopt the prescribed templates provided in the NGAS
Manual for LGU, Volumes I and II;
b. Strictly indicate the necessary information and data on the financial
reports/statement such as detailed description, unit measure, report
number, and/or remarks; and
c. Strictly reproduce the required number of copies, for each type of
report, and furnish the same to the concerned offices.

We recommended that the LCE mandate the:
a) The Local Treasurer to remit to GSIS, HDMF, PHIC, and BIR, the
exact amount of contribution and taxes withheld from
compensation as computed by the Municipal Accountant;
b) The Local Treasurer to remit the same within its prescribed period;
and
c) The Municipal Accountant to adopt measures to reconcile and
correct the over/under remittances and to determine whether the
over/under remittances were applied against employees’ accounts
or to pending penalties that resulted from the delay of remittances.

Thus, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive mandate:
a) The Accounting Office to record and account for the purchase of
welfare goods, medicines, and other supplies and materials in a
perpetual inventory method;
b) The GSO and Accounting Office to implement internal control
measures to ensure that inventories are safeguarded from
possible wastage and misappropriation; and
c) The GSO to diligently prepare, maintain and update inventory
records, forms, and reports, and consequently reconcile such
with the ledgers of the Accounting Office.

We recommended that:
a) Before payment of claims, Management must continue the strict
adherence to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, for the
documentary requirements needed on common government
transactions to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit;
and
b) Exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws, rules,
and regulations in enforcing immediate settlement of the
disallowances and suspensions under Section 7.1.1 of COA
Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Management compel the accountable officers and initiate necessary reforms in fiscal reporting and accountability. The Municipal Accountant must be required to submit promptly the periodic financial and other reports and together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor by pertinent laws, rules, and regulations to ensure timely audit of transactions and verification of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct and supervise the Municipal Accountant/Budget Officer to maintain and update these registries as per provisions outlined in Sections 18 to 22, Volume II of Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
16. Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an Assessment Roll containing the list of all persons whose real properties have been assessed for RPT;
17. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Chief Accountant with a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and
18. Municipal Accountant to properly establish and set up the RPT/SET Receivable accounts due and collectible for the year.

We recommended and urge the LCE to urge the Municipal Mayor, being the head of the agency, institutionalizes the ICS; Management compels the Municipal Officers to act and do their respective duties and functions strictly by-laws, rules, and regulations; and Direct faithful adherence to the application of internal control measures for the efficient and effective conduct of the Municipal operation towards development.

Management:
22. Ensure that full disclosure policy is exercised and observed on the PPAs under the 20% development fund and other funds for transparency and accountability purposes;
23. Submit at the start of the year, the list of all ongoing government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, with complete details; and
24. Submit regularly the monthly monitoring report on the status of PPAs under the 20% development fund.

We recommended that Management ensure that a public accountable officer who has both money and property accountability is strictly bonded to Fidelity Bond as provided per Treasury Circular No. 02-2009. The letter of the bureau of the Treasury to the Manager of the AGDB informing that the accountable officer application for fidelity bond has been submitted for approval of the Regional Director will not be considered as a bond without presenting the valid proof of bond payment.

We recommended that Management widen the allocation of the utilization of 20% of development fund to include the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development, and environment management. Rehabilitation of roads which has been ongoing since then must be lessened to give priority to viable projects that could uplift the living condition and provide livelihood to the constituents.

Top management instruct:
27. The Municipal Treasurer to issue a check with complete supporting documents for the fund transfer of unexpended LDRR M Fund from general Fund to Special Trust Fund; and
28. The Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of unexpected LDRRM Fund by drawing a JEV taking the appropriate accounting entries under General and Trust Fund.
    a. For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010).
    b. While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010) and credit the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code 2-04-01-020).

We recommended that Management ensure that payment of casuals/contractual are supported with duly verified/accepted accomplishment reports and appointments are submitted to CSC, together with other documentary requirements by Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and maintain the general and subsidiary ledgers not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111, 112, and 114 (2) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and urge the LCE to urge the Municipal SWM Boards to prepare and submit the 10 years Solid Waste Management Plan that will establish sound ecological solid waste management systems and adopted with the collaboration of the barangay officials for stricter segregation of waste.

We recommended that Management direct the treatment of travel expenses as cash advances if paid before the date of travel and liquidations and/or reimbursement must be attached with complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the LCE institute the creation of LSB in his locality and then required the constituted LSB for the preparation of the Annual School Budget Board for purposes of proper utilization and disbursement of SEF in compliance with Section 98 of RA 7160 and Item Nos. 4 and 5.2 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No 1, s. 2017.
We recommended that the management must find ways to post the bids and awards to PhilGEPS in compliance with the provisions in IRR of RA 9184.

2. Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an Assessment Roll containing the list of all persons whose real properties have been assessed for RPT; 3. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Chief Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; 4. Municipal Accountant to properly establish and set-up the RPT/SET Receivable accounts due and collectible for the year.

5. The Municipal Mayor, being the head of the agency, institutionalize the ICS; 6. Management compel the Municipal Officers to act and do their respective duties and functions strictly in accordance with laws, rules and regulations; and 7. Direct faithful adherence to the application of internal control measures for an efficient and effective conduct of the Municipal operation towards development.

8. Ensure that full disclosure policy is exercised and observed on the PPAs under the 20% development fund and other funds for transparency and accountability purpose; 9. Submit at the start of the year, the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, with complete details; and 10.Submit regularly the monthly monitoring report on the status of PPAs under the 20% development fund.

11.We recommended that Management ensure that accountable officer who has both money and property accountability are strictly bonded to Fidelity Bond as provided per Treasury Circular No. 02-2009. The letter of Bureau of the Treasury to the Manager of the AGDB informing that the accountable officer application for fidelity bond has been submitted for approval of the Regional Director will not be considered as bond without presenting the valid proof of bond payment.

16.We recommended that Management ensure that payment of casuals/contractual are supported with duly verified/accepted accomplishment reports and appointments are submitted to CSC, together with other documentary requirements in accordance to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

21.We recommended that Management furnish COA copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 within 5-days after its perfection/approval/execution for proper evaluation and remedial measures on possible defects will not be too late to enforce.

27.Must made sure that the conditions and requirements prescribed in Appendix I of RA 9184 are duly complied with prior to the implementation/construction of any project to be undertaken by administration; 28.Must provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable; and 29.Install safety and security measures so that the life and property of any prospective contractors can properly be safeguarded.

33.We recommended that management should ensure that due notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

34.We recommended that management should make sure that observers (consisting of the concerned auditor, a representative of a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand and a representative of a NGO) are duly notified in writing at least 2-day before the stages of procurement to which observers shall be invited, namely: pre-bid conference; opening of bids; post-qualification; contract award; and special meetings of the BAC.

1.5 We therefore recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a) Immediately create the Inventory Committee, composed of
adequate number of members, and complete the physical count in
three (3) months or less and submit a report thereon. The
Inventory Committee shall have at least one member each from
the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units of the agency;
b) Instruct the Inventory Committee to plan/strategize on how to
conduct and complete the physical inventory within the
prescribed period by preparing a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP)
duly approved by the Mayor, containing the specific
assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date
and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory
taking activities from start up to the targeted completion of
physical inventory;
c) Thereafter, the Inventory Committee shall submit the approved
PIP to the Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the
schedule start of inventory; and
d) Instruct the General Services Officer to update the property
records based on the result of the physical count and reconcile the
same with the accounting records to come up with correct
balances of PPE accounts.

2.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following to the
concerned Municipal Officers:
a) Municipal Engineer – Provide the local accountant and the general
service officer with the complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects and the water supply system;
b) General Service Officer – Keep a complete Property Card to all the
infrastructure assets and maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road
Map;
c) Municipal Accountant –
i. Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning
balance of the local road network and its components in the
general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card;
ii. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures on the
corresponding accounts of Local Road Network and the Water
Supply System. Work on the distribution of cost for road
components and the water supply system and determine the
corresponding depreciated replacement cost for road
components with no available cost per registry;
iii. Keep and maintain subsidiary records to roads and road
components and the water supply system;
iv. Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the
depreciation for each infrastructures account at the end of
the year.

3.6 We recommended that the Management compel the accountable
officers and initiate necessary reforms in fiscal reporting and
accountability. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
must be required to submit promptly the periodic financial and other
reports together with the supporting documents to the concerned local
auditor in accordance to pertinent laws, rules and regulations to ensure
timely audit of transactions and verification of accounts.

4.7 We recommended that:
a) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
continuously effect the timely and accurate mandatory
contributions to GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and income tax
within the prescribed rate and period of remittances;
b) The discrepancies from the previous years’ related accounts per
financial statement must be thoroughly reviewed and reconciled
to present the accurate status of contribution and remittance; and
c) The employees’ ledger cards must be set-up completely and
regularly maintained for easier reconciliation and monitoring of
the actual status of accounts of LGUs and the employees share.

5.3 We recommended that:
a) Prior to payment of claims, Management must strictly adhere to
COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the documentary
requirements needed on common government transactions to
avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit; and
b) Exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws, rules
and regulations in enforcing immediate settlement of the
disallowances and suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA
Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

1. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct and supervise the Municipal Accountant/Budget Officer to maintain and update these registries as per provisions set-forth in Sections 18 to 22, Volume II of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs).

12.We recommended that Management widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to include the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic and environment management. Rehabilitation of roads which has been on-going since then, must be lessen to give priority to viable projects that could provide livelihood to the constituents.

13.The Municipal Treasurer to issue a check with complete supporting documents for the transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund from general Fund to Special Trust Fund; and 14.The Municipal Account to record the transfer of unexpected LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the appropriate accounting entries under General and Trust Fund. a. For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010). b. While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010) and credit the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code 2-04-01-020).

15.To achieve the GAD Program implementation objectives and gender mainstream, we recommended that GAD funds must be judicially and efficiently used and further substantially supported with GAD related reports as well as monitoring and evaluation report as in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

17.We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and maintain the General and subsidiary ledgers not only on accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111, 112 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

18.We recommended and urge the Local Chief Executive to urge the Municipal Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards to prepare and submit the 10 years Solid Waste Management Plan that will establish sound ecological solid waste management systems and adopted with the collaboration of the barangay officials for stricter segregation of waste.

19.We recommended that Management direct the treatment of travel expenses as cash advances if paid before the date of travel and liquidations and/or reimbursement must be attached with complete supporting documents.

20.We recommended that Municipal Government determine their insurable assets and adequately cover it with GSIS insurance to indemnify the assets in case of damage/loss due to fire, earthquake and other fortuitous event.

22.We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute the creation of Local School Board (LSB) in his locality and then required the constituted LSB for the preparation of Annual School Budget Board for purposes of utilization and disbursement of Special Education Fund (SEF) in compliance with Section 98 of RA 7160 and Item Nos. 4 and 5.2 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

23.The Implementing Agency (IA) to which the funds are transferred for the purpose of prosecuting/ implementing the project should faithfully perform the duties and responsibilities enumerated in Item 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

24.The Municipal officials must be aware that any financial assistance, contribution and other disbursements to Leagues of Provinces, Municipalities, Barangays, and Federation of Leagues must be properly accounted, recorded and utilized in accordance to laws, rules and regulations. Such collective funds from government source involved material amount that if not audited may go astray and subjected to misuse.

25.We recommended that management initiate measures designed to come up with a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the GSIS defining therein the agreed balance of payables to GSIS, if any, and the terms and conditions in the settlement thereof. The following steps may help in facilitating reconciliation of records. a. Secure detailed profile of members-employees of payables per GSIS records; b. Reconstruct old GSIS accounts, if necessary, and update records; c. Reconstruct old personnel profile, if necessary, and update personnel profile; d. Counter-check GSIS records with the reconstructed and updated accounting and personnel records of the LGU; e. Negotiate with the GSIS for any differences that may be noted during counter-checking activity; f. Enter into a formal agreement with the GSIS in the form of a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and g. Appropriate fund for the settlement of any payables to the GSIS as agreed in the MOA.
26.We recommended that the management must find ways to post the bids and awards to PhilGEPS in compliance to provisions in IRR of RA 9184.

30.Demonstrating leadership by example; 31.Training personnel to gain work competence and or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and 32.Maintaining a complete set of up-to-date records, namely: a. Journals; b. Ledgers; c. Registries; and d. Cash records.

3. We recommended that the top management initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.
4. Specifically, the LCE should direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the periodic financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents so that timely verification thereof could be facilitated.
5. Consequently, management could be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected so that timely corrections/adjustments thereof can be affected to their own advantage.

6. We recommended that Management ensure that public accountable officer who has both money and property accountability are strictly bonded to Fidelity Bond as provided per Treasury Circular No. 02-2009. The letter of Bureau of the Treasury to the Manager of the AGDB informing that the accountable officer application for fidelity bond has been submitted for approval of the Regional Director will not be considered as bond without presenting the valid proof of bond payment.

7. We recommended that Management widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to include the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management. Rehabilitation of roads which has been on-going since then, must be lessen to give priority to viable projects that could uplift the living condition and provide livelihood outcomes to the constituents.

8. The Municipal Treasurer to issue a check with complete supporting documents for the transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund from general Fund to Special Trust Fund;
9. The Municipal Account to record the transfer of unexpected LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the appropriate accounting entries under General and Trust Fund.
    a. For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010).
    b. While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010) and credit the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code 2-04-01-020).

10. To achieve the GAD Program objectives and gender mainstream, we recommended that GAD funds must be judicially and efficiently used and further substantially supported with GAD related reports as well as monitoring and evaluation report as in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

11. We recommended that Management ensure that payment of casuals/contractual are supported with duly verified/accepted accomplishment reports and appointments are submitted to CSC, together with other documentary requirements in accordance to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

12. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and maintain the General and subsidiary ledgers not only on accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111, and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

16. We recommended that Management furnish COA copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 within 5-days after its perfection/approval/execution for proper evaluation and remedial measures on possible defects will not be too late to enforce.

17. We recommended that Management submit the List of all on-going Projects/PPAs to COA at the on-set of the year to promote transparency and good governance and stakeholders were able to monitor the status of the PPAs of the municipality.

29. We recommended that management should ensure that notification to the Auditor of time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

30. We recommended that management should make sure that observers (consisting of the concerned auditor, a representative of a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to procurement at hand and a representative of a NGO) are duly notified in writing at least 2-day before the stages of procurement to which observers shall be invited, namely: pre-bid conference; opening of bids; post-qualification; contract award; and special meetings of the BAC.

1.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the conduct of a thorough and complete physical count of all properties
of the Municipality; and
b) Require the submission of a complete Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE).

2.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct and supervise the
Municipal Accountant/Budget Officer to maintain and update these
registries as per provisions set-forth in Sections 18 to 22, Volume II of
Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

3.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an
Assessment Roll containing the list of all persons whose real properties have
been assessed for RPT;
b) Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Chief Accountant of a duly certified list
showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the
year; and
c) Municipal Accountant to properly establish and set-up the RPT/SET
Receivable accounts due and collectible for the year.

4.9 We recommended that:
a) The Municipal Mayor, being the head of the agency, institutionalize
the ICS;
b) Management compel the Municipal Officers to act and do their
respective duties and functions strictly in accordance with laws,
rules and regulations; and
c) Direct faithful adherence to the application of internal control
measures for an efficient and effective conduct of the Municipal
operation towards development.

5.5 We recommended that Management:
a) Ensure that full disclosure policy is exercised and observed on the
PPAs under the 20% development fund and other funds for
transparency and accountability purpose;
b) Submit at the start of the year, the list of all on-going government
PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, with
complete details; and
c) Submit regularly the monthly monitoring report on the status of
PPAs under the 20% development fund.

6.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer continuously effect the timely and accurate mandatory
contributions to GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and income tax within the
prescribed rate and period of remittances. The discrepancies on the
previous years’ related accounts per financial statements must be
thoroughly reviewed and reconciled to present an accurate status of
contribution and remittance. The employees’ ledger cards must be set-
up completely and regularly maintained for easier reconciliation and
monitoring of the actual status of accounts of LGUs and the employees’
share.

7.4 We recommended that:
a) Prior to payment of claims, management must strictly adhere to
COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the documentary
requirements needed on common government transactions to
avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit; and
b) Exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws, rules
and regulations in enforcing immediate settlement of the
disallowances and suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA
Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

1. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents on the total amount ₱7,812,810.21 unsubmitted transactions for proper evaluation and adjustments be made on the financial statements to correct presentation of the expense account.
2. All payments against the depository account of the municipality must be paid through check issuance with proper attachment of Disbursement Voucher with complete signatories of the approving officers supported with valid documents.

13. We recommended and urge the Local Chief Executive to urge the Municipal Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards to prepare and submit the 10 years Solid Waste Management Plan that will establish sound ecological solid waste management systems and adopted with the collaboration of the barangay officials for stricter segregation of waste.

14. We recommended that Management direct the treatment of travel expenses as cash advances if paid before the date of travel and liquidations and/or reimbursement must be attached with complete supporting documents.

15. We recommended that Municipal Government to determine their insurable assets and adequately cover it with GSIS insurance to indemnify the assets in case of damage/loss due to fire, earthquake and other fortuitous event.

18. 19. The Implementing Agency (IA) to which the funds are transferred for the purpose of prosecuting/ implementing the project should faithfully perform the duties and responsibilities enumerated in Item 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

Such collective funds from government source involved material amount that if not audited may go astray and subjected to misuse.

We recommended that Management ensure that payment of casuals/contractual are strictly supported with duly verified/accepted accomplishment reports and appointments are submitted to CSC.
observations.managementAction: The LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to comply the requirements together with other documentary requirements in accordance to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.reason: Management did not provide the reason why.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer for the implementation of the accounts and maintain reporting of subsidiary ledgers not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111, 112 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The LCE must require the Municipal Treasurer for the implementation of the accounts and maintain reporting of subsidiary ledgers not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the treatment of travel expenses as cash advances if paid before the date of travel and that travel liquidations and/or reimbursement must be attached with complete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The LCE must direct the Municipal accountant and treasurer to strictly observed that travel expenses must be incurred in accordance to COA Circ. 2012-001.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management furnish COA copy of any contract/purchase order and its supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 within 5-days after its perfection/approval/execution for proper evaluation and remedial measures on possible defects.
observations.managementAction: The LCE must direct the Municipal accountant and treasurer to strictly observed the furnishing of contracts & POs to COA within prescribed period of time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the List of PPAs to COA at the on-set of the year to promote transparency of the PPAs so that stakeholders were able to monitor the status of the PPAs of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and Municipal Engineer must submit every start of the year the on-going and those that are to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Agency (IA) to which the funds are transferred for the purpose of prosecuting/implementing the project should faithfully perform their duties and responsibilities implementing the project from other sources.
observations.managementAction: The LCE must direct the Municipal accountant and treasurer to strictly observed the duties and responsibility in implementing the project from other sources.

observations.recommendation:
1.12 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit
the supporting documents on the total amount of ₱7,812,810.21 unsubmitted
transactions for proper evaluation and adjustments be made on the financial
statements to correct presentation of the expense account. All payments against
the depository account of the municipality must be paid through check issuance
with proper attachment of Disbursement Voucher with complete signatories of the
approving officers supported with valid documents.

1.13 We further recommendeded that government transactions must not be paid or
withdrawn as cash withdrawals in the depository account for transparency reason
and avoid the risk of possible loss of fund.

2.9 We recommended that the top management initiate necessary reforms for fiscal
reporting and accountability by enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and
regulations on the rendition of financial reports. Specifically, the LCE should
direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the
periodic financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents so that
timely verification thereof could be facilitated. Consequently, management
could be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected so that timely
corrections/adjustments thereof can be affected to their own advantage.

4.7 We recommended that Management ensure that public accountable officer who has
both money and property accountability are strictly bonded to Fidelity Bond as
provided per Treasury Circular No. 02-2009. The letter of Bureau of the Treasury
to the Manager of the AGDB informing that the accountable officer application for
fidelity bond had been submitted for approval of the Regional Director will not be
consider as bond without presenting the valid proof of bond payment.

5.6 We recommendeded that Management widen the allocation of utilization of 20%
development fund to include the three priority programs and projects such as
social development, economic development and environment management.
Rehabilitation of roads which has been on-going since then, must be lessen to give
priority to viable projects that could uplift the living condition and provide
livelihood to the constituents.

6.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the conduct of a thorough
and complete physical count of all properties of the municipality. Require the
update of inventory reports every end of the year. The accounting and property
office must comply with the prescribed policies and procedures in the recording,
reporting and maintenance of records of PPE account.

7.7 We recommended that Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
continuously effect the timely and accurate mandatory contributions to GSIS,
PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and income tax within the prescribed rate and period of
remittances. The discrepancies on the previous year related accounts per financial
statement must be thoroughly reviewed and reconcile to present an accurate status
of contribution and remittance. The employees’ ledger cards must be set-up
completely and regularly maintain for an easier reconciliation and monitoring of
the actual status of accounts of LGUs and the employees share.

8.6 We recommended that top management instruct:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to issue a check with complete supporting
documents for the fund transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund from General
Fund to Special Trust Fund, and
b. The Municipal Account to record the transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund
by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the appropriate accounting
entries under General and Trust Fund.
c. For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of Unspent Current
Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (Account Code 5-02-15-010) and
credit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account
(Account Code 1-01-02-010).
d. While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank – Local
Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010) and credit the
account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
(Account Code 2-04-01-020).

9.6 To achieve the GAD Program objectives and gender mainstream, we recommended
that GAD funds must be judicially and efficiently used and further substantially
supported with GAD related reports as well as monitoring and evaluation report
as in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2013-01.

10.3 We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies provided
under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate settlement of the
disallowances and suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No.
2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. It should be emphasized that prior to
payment of claims, that management must strictly adhere to COA Circular 2012-
001 dated June 14, 2012 for the documentary requirements needed on common
government transactions before payment to avoid suspensions and/or disallowance
in audit.

We recommended and urge the Local Chief Executive to urge the Municipal Solid Waste Management Boards to prepare the Solid Waste Management Plan to prepare the Solid Waste Management Plan and adopt a sound ecological solid waste management system.
observations.managementAction: LCE convene with the SB for the creation of Boards to prepare and submit the 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan to establish a sound ecological solid waste management system.
observations.reason: Segregation of wastes have been adopted with the collaboration of the barangay officials for stricter segregation of waste.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government determine their insurable assets and adequately cover it with GSIS insurance to indemnify the assets in case of the damage/loss due to fire, earthquake and other fortuitous event.
observations.managementAction: The LCE must direct the Municipal accountant and treasurer to adequately insure GSIS in covering insurable assets of LGU.
observations.reason: There are problems encountered on GSIS in covering insurable assets of LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute the creation of Local School Board (LSB) in his locality and then constituted LSB for the preparation of Annual School Budget Board for purposes of proper utilization and disbursement of Special Education Fund (SEF) in compliance with Section 98 of RA 7160 and Item Nos. 4 and 5.2 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: The LCE convene with the SB for the creation of Local School Board for proper utilization of SEF.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management must be aware that any financial assistance, contribution and other disbursements to Leagues of Provinces, Municipalities, Barangays, Federation of Leagues must be properly accounted, recorded and utilized in accordance to laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor any payments for Leagues of Provinces, Municipalities, Barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should initiate measures designed to come up with a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the GSIS defining therein the agreed balance of payables to GSIS, if any, the terms and conditions in the settlement thereof. The following steps may help in facilitating current reconciliation of records: a. Secure detailed profile of payables per GSIS records; b. Reconstruct old GSIS accounts, if necessary, and update records; c. Reconstruct old personnel profile, if necessary, and update personnel profile; d. Counter-check GSIS records with the reconstructed and updated accounting and personnel records of the LGU; e. Negotiate with the GSIS for any differences that may be noted during counter-checking activity; f. Enter into a formal agreement with the GSIS in the form of a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and g. Appropriate fund for the settlement of any payables to the GSIS as agreed in the MOA.
observations.managementAction: Management is in process of reconciling the accounts. The Municipal Accountant coordinated with GSIS to reconcile the accounts.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of accounts with GSIS is still in process before finalizing the Memorandum of Agreement.

observations.recommendation: It was recommended that the management must find ways to post the bids and awards to PhilGEPS in compliance to the provisions in IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Direct the Municipal Accountant and Bid Award Committee to publish in the PhilGEPS the invitation to apply and/or bid.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management: must make sure that the conditions and requirements prescribed in Appendix I of RA 9184 are duly complied with prior to the implementation/construction of any project to be undertaken by administration; must provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable; and install safety and security measures so that the life and property of any prospective contractors can properly be safeguarded.
observations.managementAction: To be implemented

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal accountability by: Demonstrating leadership by example; Training personnel to gain work competence and or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; Maintaining a complete set of up-to-date records, namely: Journals; Ledgers; Registries; and Cash records.
observations.managementAction: The LCE call had a meeting with the accountable officer for the discussion of the recommendation and look for other options.
observations.reason: Management did not offer an explanation to this.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should make sure that due notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.
observations.managementAction: Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to notify COA on every scheduled stages of the procurement.
observations.reason: Management did not offer an explanation to this.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should make sure that observers (consisting of the concerned auditor, a BAC representative of a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline of procurement at hand and a representative of a NGO) are duly notified in writing at least 2-day before the stages of procurement to which observers shall be invited, namely: pre-bid conference; opening of bids; post-qualification; contract award; and special meetings of the BAC.
observations.managementAction: Direct the Municipal Accountant/Treasurer and BAC to notify COA on every scheduled stages of procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should strictly enforce the operational mandates of the Office of the Municipal Assessor in particular to the assessment of real property for the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to come up with a realistic tax valuation of real property for use as valid input in the books and serving as basis in enforcing collections of RPT and SEF receivables.
observations.managementAction: Management is in process of informing the constituents regarding the RPT.

observations.recommendation: It was recommended that management: Must initiate to obtain internet access to facilitate internet publication of procurements, such as inviting possible providers to install internet services in the community; Make some pre-arrangements with newspaper media of general nationwide circulation in a way that could facilitate newspaper publications incurring less costs, time and efforts for the LGU, i.e. through phone call, text message or e-mail; Provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had conducted procurement/acquisition of heavy equipment funded from domestic loan. However the rest of the project implementation and other purchase of materials was not adequately advertised.

observations.recommendation: The Implementing Agency (IA) to which the funds are transferred for the purpose of prosecuting/ implementing the project should faithfully perform the duties and responsibilities enumerated in Item 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: The LCE must direct the Municipal accountant and treasurer to strictly observed their duties and responsibility in implementing the project from other sources.

observations.recommendation: Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instruct the MPDC to coordinate with the DENR and the barangays for sound ecological solid waste  programs and projects that will be installed in the locality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management must use the earmarked GAD Funds in line with the purposes of (a) promoting gender responsive governance; (b) protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights; and (c) promoting women’s economic empowerment.  Moreover, copy of the annual accomplishment report of GAD program, project and activities vis-à-vis with target must be submitted for qualitative evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The LCE enquire the focal GAD coordinator  the GAD Plans and Programs and had meeting with the municipal administrator for the conduct of GAD activities and proper documentation of the activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management must find ways to post the bids and awards to PhilGEPS in compliance to the provisions in IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Management did not comment on this.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that payment of casuals/contractual are supported with duly verified/accepted accomplishment reports and appointments are submitted to CSC, together with other documentary requirements in accordance to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Top Management compel the accountable officers and initiate necessary reforms in fiscal reporting and accountability.  The Municipal Accountant must be required to submit promptly the periodic financial and other reports and together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor in accordance to pertinent laws, rules and regulations to ensure timely audit of transactions and verification of accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and maintain the General and subsidiary ledgers not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111, 112 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that Management widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to include the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.  Rehabilitation of roads which has been on-going since then, must be lessen to give priority to viable projects that could uplift the living condition and provide livelihood to the constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the conduct of a thorough and complete physical count of all properties of the municipality. Require the update of inventory reports every end of the year.   The accounting and property office must comply with the prescribed policies and procedures in the recording, reporting and maintenance of records of PPE account.

We recommended and urge the Local Chief Executive to urge the Municipal Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards to prepare and submit the 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan that will establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and adopted with the collaboration of the barangay officials for  stricter segregation of waste.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continuously effect a timely and accurate mandatory contributions to GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and income tax within the prescribed rate and period of remittances.  The discrepancies on the previous year related accounts per financial statement must be thoroughly reviewed and reconcile to present an accurate status of contribution and remittance.  Complete employees ledger cards must be set-up and maintained for an easier reconciliation and monitoring of the actual status of accounts of LGUs and the employees share.

We recommended that Management direct the treatment of travel expenses as cash advances if paid before the date of travel and liquidations and/or reimbursement must be attached with complete supporting documents.

We recommended that Management follow the LDRRM existing rules and regulations in the reporting, booking and utilization of the LDRRM Funds.  The operational structures, system and policies must be strongly in place and followed and applied in case of disaster.  All activities related to mitigation of disaster, starting from preparedness, information dissemination, knowing the extent of damage and number of victims and their needs, procurement and distribution of aid must be well documented and properly reported.

We recommended that Municipal Government determine their insurable assets and adequately cover it with GSIS insurance to indemnify the assets in case of damage/loss due to fire, earthquake and other fortuitous event.

To strengthen the GAD Focal Point System of the LGU, we recommended that the Management use the earmarked GAD Funds in line with the purposes of (a) promoting gender responsive governance; (b) protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights; and (c) promoting women’s economic empowerment.  Moreover, copy of the annual accomplishment report of GAD program, project and activities vis-à-vis with target must be submitted for qualitative evaluation.

We recommended that Management submit the List of PPAs to COA at the on-set of the year to promote transparency and good governance and stakeholders were be able to monitor the status of the PPAs of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute the creation of Local School Board (LSB) in his locality and then required the constituted LSB for the preparation of Annual School Budget Board for purposes of proper utilization and disbursement of Special Education Fund (SEF) in compliance with Section 98 of RA 7160 and Item Nos. 4 and 5.2 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that Management furnish COA copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 within 5-days after its perfection/approval/execution for proper evaluation and remedial measures on possible defects will not be too late to enforce.

We recommended that officers/employees determined to be responsible submit immediately the required documents and/or payments for the settlement of the suspension/disallowances.  It should be emphasized that prior to payment of claims, that management must strictly adhere to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the documentary requirements needed on common government transactions before payment to avoid suspensions and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the accountant must thoroughly process, check the computation of payrolls and require all concerned official and employee to attach the individual daily time record including the other prescribed documentary requirements before payment is made.  It was further recommended that payment of salaries must be done through Automotive Teller Machine (ATM) to lessen the grant of cash advances and avoid the possibility of payroll padding and doubts of the existence of the person who actually received the net pay.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the Mun. Acctnt. to thoroughly check the computation of payroll and enquire LBP for possible payment of salaries through ATM.
observations.reason: Management had enquired with the PNB for the payment of salary through ATM (Automotive Teller Machine) and said they need more time to process the request and it depends upon the number of employees.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal officials must be aware that any financial assistance, contribution and other disbursements to Leagues of Provinces, Municipalities, Barangays, Federation of Leagues must be properly accounted, recorded and utilized in accordance to laws, rules and regulations.  Such collective funds from government source involved material amount that if not audited may go astray and subjected to misuse.
observations.managementAction: The LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor any payments for  financial assistance, contribution and other disbursements to Leagues of Provinces, Municipalities, Barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF must be prepared, certified correct by the Accountant submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Auditor’s Office.  Moreover, any unexpended balance must be maintained on separate account “Trust Liability-DRRM.”
observations.reason: Submitted the December 2016 Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

Reconciliation of the records and accounts with GSIS is still in process before finalizing the Memorandum of Agreement.  Actual rate of deductions and remittances must be effect for the regular and current personnel of the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that management:
  Must made sure that the conditions and requirements prescribed in Appendix I of RA 9184 are duly complied with prior to the implementation/construction of any project to be undertaken by administration;
  Must provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable; and
  Install safety and security measures so that the life and property of any prospective contractors can properly be safeguarded.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that:
They must be oriented on laws, rules and regulations concerning hiring/employment of contractual workers and strictly adhere thereof. 
Specifically, make sure that claims for wages of casual and contractual workers are duly supported with necessary papers pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 and other pertinent guidelines.  
Moreover, be guided with the provisions of RA 9184 on the procurement of manpower which provides under Appendix I (3.7) thereof that the manual labor component of projects undertaken by administration may be undertaken (1) in-house (personnel occupying regular plantilla), (2) by job order or through the pakyaw contracting system.

We recommended that management should make sure that observers (consisting of the concerned auditor, a representative of a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand and a representative of a NGO) are duly notified in writing at least 2-day before the stages of procurement to which observers shall be invited, namely: pre-bid conference; opening of bids; post-qualification; contract award; and special meetings of the BAC.
observations.reason: Management did not offer an explanation to this

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should strictly enforce the operational mandates of the Office of the Municipal Assessor in particular to the assessment of real property for the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to come up with a realistic tax valuation of real property for use as valid input in the books and serving as basis in enforcing collections of RPT and SEF receivables.

It was recommended that management:
Must initiate to obtain internet access to facilitate internet publication of procurements, such as inviting possible internet providers to install internet services in the community.  
Make some pre-arrangements with newspaper media of general nationwide circulation in a way that could facilitate newspaper publications incurring less costs, time and efforts for the LGU, i.e. through phone call, text message or e-mail. 
Provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable.
We recommend to prepare a correct Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit it on time for audit.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliations submitted.
observations.reason: Accountant agreed that proper format will be followed.

observations.recommendation: Management should strictly observe Section 4 (6 & 7) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 (PD 1445) and paragraph 4.1.3 of COA Circular 2012-001and other applicable laws, rules and regulations in the processing of claim against government funds and  disbursements should always be supported with proper documentation to determine propriety and legality of said claims.
observations.reason: Management did not offer an explanation to this.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that in all matters involving contract of loans for acquisition of properties, the concerned officials are advised to submit a copy thereof to the auditor and notify the auditor upon delivery thereof. Strict adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 89-299, in order for the auditor to have ample time to review the pertinent contract.
observations.managementAction: The loan had been fully paid.
observations.reason: Municipal officials explained that they have not kept a copy of the loans granted and negotiated by previous administrations.  Request and query for copy of loan portfolio was sent to the branch which they forwarded to the Head Office since the processing and approval of loans are was done Head Branch.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should prepare and submit to the local sanggunian, through the LDRRMC and the LDC, the annual LDRRMO Plan and Budget, the proposed programming of the LDRRMF, other dedicated disaster risk reduction and management resources and other regular funding source/s and budgetary support of the LDRRMO/BDRRMO.
observations.managementAction: Management will submit the plan and budget of the LDRRM this CY 2016
observations.reason: Management explained that though they have not planned, the council will always been on hand and ready for any risk and disaster.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Office should adhere strictly with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2005 of the DILG and DBM and the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the Annual Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for development projects.
observations.reason: The MPDC did not justify why road rehabilitation has been their priority program in the 20% utilization.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular No. 2009-01 dated February 12, 2009, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the mode of procurement.
observations.reason: Management did not offer an explanation to this.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that management under the initiative of the Municipal Mayor, should complete the conduct of physical inventory and prepare inventory reports of all their properties.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Administrator with the property custodian agreed that inventory and proper listing will be conducted this CY 2015.
observations.reason: Management reasoned that the changes of accountable officers with improper turnover of documents and properties affected the inventory of PPE.

We recommend that the accountant must thoroughly process, check the computation of payrolls and require all concerned official and employee to attach the individual daily time record including the other prescribed documentary requirements before payment is made.
It was further recommended that payment of salaries must be done through Automotive Teller Machine (ATM) to lessen the grant of cash advances and avoid the possibility of payroll padding and doubts of the existence of the person who actually received the net pay.

2.13  It is recommended that the top management must compel the accountable officers and initiate necessary reforms in fiscal reporting and accountability by:
demonstrating leadership by example;
training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and
enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.
2.14 It is further recommended that top management should direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the periodic financial and other reports and together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the top management could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected and corrections /adjustments thereof could be effected.

3.5  Required the concerned Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and subsidiary records not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

4.5  It is recommended that the management should widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to include the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.  Rehabilitation of roads which has been on-going since then, must be lessen to give priority to viable projects that could uplift the living condition and provide livelihood to the constituents.

5.5  The Implementing Agency (IA) to which the funds are transferred for the purpose of prosecuting/implementing the project should faithfully perform the duties and responsibilities enumerated in Item 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

6.6  Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

7.6  It is recommended that the management should continue to deduct and remit all mandatory contributions for GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and income tax within the prescribed period of remittances.  And further review the deductions made from the salary of the  employees which must be in accordance to the prescribed rate stated in applicable laws, rules and regulations on income tax, GSIS, Pag-ibig and PhilHealth contributions.

8.6  We recommended that the Local Chief Executive must direct the conduct of a thorough and complete physical inventory taking of all properties of the municipality. Require the update of inventory reports of all offices/department every year end which must be further reconcile with the PPE account.  Adjustments must be affected to establish the correct balance of PPE per books and per subsidiary ledgers.

9.4  The Municipal officials must be aware that any financial assistance, contribution and other disbursements to Leagues of Provinces, Municipalities, Barangays, Federation of Leagues must be properly accounted, recorded and utilized in accordance to laws, rules and regulations.  Such collective funds from government source involved material amount that if not audited may go astray and subjected to misuse.

10.7  It is recommended that municipal government must determine their insurable assets and adequately cover it with insurance to indemnify the assets in case of damage/loss due to fire, earthquake and other fortuitous event.

12.6  We recommended that the management must use the earmarked GAD Funds in line with the purposes of (a) promoting gender responsive governance; (b) protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights; and (c) promoting women’s economic empowerment.  Moreover, copy of the annual accomplishment report of GAD program, project and activities vis-à-vis with target must be submitted for qualitative evaluation.

  Install safety and security measures so that the life and property of any prospective contractors can properly be safeguarded.

We recommended the immediate creation of an inventory committee that will conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all properties of the municipality and require update of inventory reports of all offices/department as of December 31, 2015.  We further recommended that the inventory reports must be reconciled with the reported PPE accounts and adjustments must be affected to establish the correct balance per books.
observations.managementAction: Had created an Inventory Committee and physical inventory had been initially conducted and reconciliation is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer must submit within five (5) days after the end of each month, the monthly RCD together with the supporting official receipts and validated deposit slips to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording in the books of accounts and to ensure that the said reports be submitted to the Auditor within ten (10) days from receipt.
observations.managementAction: Transactions was being work back from the source documents for proper recording and adjusting entries will be done if necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF must be prepared, certified correct by the Accountant submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Auditor’s Office.  Moreover, any unexpended balance must be maintained on separate account “Trust Liability-DRRM.”
observations.reason: Submitted the December 2016 Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

Reconciliation of the records and accounts with GSIS is still in process before finalizing the Memorandum of Agreement.  Actual rate of deductions and remittances must be effect for the regular and current personnel of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local officials must give priority in the preparation and approval of its Local Government Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan as required under RA 9003.  Proper implementation of the plan involving the community will result into a clean and healthy public and environment.
observations.reason: Created Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, who will prepare the 10 year Local Solid Waste Management Plan.
